heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 110–40, § 1(a)121 Stat. 232June 29, 2007
label: 1401 to 1401e Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 110–40, § 1(a)121 Stat. 232June 29, 2007
notes: Section 1401, , , set out the policy of Congress to equalize taxes on real property in the Virgin Islands.act May 26, 1936, ch. 450, § 149 Stat. 1372
Section 1401a, , , related to valuation of real property for assessment and uniformity of rates.act May 26, 1936, ch. 450, § 249 Stat. 1372

Section 1401b, , , related to rate of tax in absence of local laws and regulations by President for assessment and collection pending adoption of local laws.act May 26, 1936, ch. 450, § 349 Stat. 1372
Section 1401c, , , provided that taxes were to be deposited in the municipal treasury of the municipality in which collected.act May 26, 1936, ch. 450, § 449 Stat. 1372

Section 1401d, acts , ; , , related to payments to be made by the Virgin Islands Corporation into municipal treasuries of the Virgin Islands in lieu of certain taxes, valuation of real property in the Virgin Islands owned by the Virgin Islands Corporation as a basis for determining the amount of taxation, and payment to be made for any property owned by the United States in the Virgin Islands used for ordinary business or commercial purposes.May 26, 1936, ch. 450, § 549 Stat. 1372June 30, 1949, ch. 285, § 1263 Stat. 356
Section 1401e, , , related to exemptions from taxation and authority of municipalities to alter, amend, or repeal existing laws.act May 26, 1936, ch. 450, § 649 Stat. 1373

Effective Date of Repeal, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–40, § 1(b)121 Stat. 232June 29, 2007“This section [repealing sections 1401 to 1401e of this title] shall be deemed to have taken effect on .”July 22, 1954
